title: A Day in the Life of a Flight Student
body: PSA: This is NOT what flight training looks like at all. That is flight training at a part 141 academy that takes things WAY to seriously. Training part 61 is as simple as showing up, and flying a plane. A decent 141 school is showing up, and flying the plane, along with separate days doing ground classes and other college courses for your degree. Only bad academy’s force students to do their ground studies before flying. Any decent school will have EVERY student in the air flying planes within their first week at school.
author: @DaCatmasterX

title: A Day in the Life of a Flight Student
body: PPL is affordable. CPL is where the money problem starts. I’m 32 and will be embarking on my flying journey this year after working for a decade saving up money.
author: @tarvinderpal_S

title: A Day in the Life of a Flight Student
body:
So glad I went the part 61 cowboy route and bought a Cessna 150 and slapped a 50k panel and new interior in it and did it all with a private CFI/CFIii
Half the price as one of these flight schools and I got to keep the airplane when I was done haha

title: A Day in the Life of a Flight Student
body:
…you also forgot about the $90k student loan you’re going to need to take out, in order to go to the school. That is assuming you get all your certifications; PPL, IR, MEI, CFI, etc..Also you would probably need your 1st class Medical as well.
Great school none the less 😎

body: Currently attending and am currently sitting on the toilet shitting my guts out after eating at the oaks. Legit gastroenterological torture.
author: @NoelTaylor42

title: Bowling Green State University - Campus Tour
body: Thanks for the virtual tour! I haven't been back to campus in years so enjoyed seeing more recent buildings as well as those like the library - I remember well that walk up the ramp... BGSU was always progressive and innovative and still is; I liked seeing those delivery vehicles in action! It looks like this was recorded at the time of year when leaves have fallen and grass has turned brown, which may not be the most scenic time of year. And, it's flat! very flat. I loved my years at BG and got a great education. Ay Ziggy Zoomba!
author: @sharona9028
